mods.title: Mods
mods.openRegistryWebsite: Open the BeamMP Mod Registry website
mods.registryHelpBody: The BeamMP Mod Registry is a community-curated index of mods. Authors and users can record metadata, version compatibility, and install hints — making mods easier to find, install, and keep working across servers. Sign in to contribute or vouch for what works.
mods.classifyModScope: How will this mod be used?
mods.classifyModScopeDesc: Classify "{{name}}" so it can be deployed correctly to servers.
mods.scopeClientOnlyDesc: Standard BeamNG mod — vehicles, maps, UI apps, etc.
mods.scopeClientServerDesc: Zip contains Resources/Client/ and Resources/Server/ trees
mods.scopeServerOnlyDesc: Server plugin with Lua scripts (e.g. CobaltEssentials)
mods.sideloadMultiplayerWarning: Sideloading mods before joining multiplayer lobbies can cause issues and result in a poor experience for other players.
mods.dependsOn: The following mods depend on this:
mods.repairIndexTooltip: Fix stale or duplicate mod db entries, then rescan installed mods
mods.modsWillBeRemoved: The following mods will be removed:
mods.enforcementTooltip: Rename mod files with numeric prefixes so the game loads them in this order
mods.scanConflictsTooltip: Scan active mods for overlapping files to see which mod wins

friends.title: Friends
friends.description: This feature is under development. The friends system will let you:
friends.addFriendsDesc: Players you've driven with will appear as suggestions
friends.seeOnlineDesc: Check which server your friends are playing on
friends.futureIntegrationsDesc: BeamMP friend lists or BeamNG follow system when available
friends.tailscaleDesc: See friends on your Tailnet and join their servers directly via private network
friends.beammpRequestsDescription: Mutual friend requests require a friends API from the BeamMP backend. This feature is reserved for when BeamMP exposes an official friend request system.
friends.featureMutualAcceptance: Mutual acceptance before appearing on each other's list
friends.featureWaitingBackend: Waiting for an official friends API endpoint from the BeamMP team
friends.noSuggestionsYet: No suggestions yet. Play on servers and we'll suggest players you've been on servers with multiple times.
friends.addFriendsDescription: Add friends by their BeamMP username to track when they're online.
friends.noOnlineFriends: None of your friends are online right now.
friends.enterBeamMPUsername: Enter their BeamMP username exactly as it appears in-game.

controls.perDeviceDesc: Detect connected devices and manage bindings per device with deadzone and linearity controls
controls.axesComingSoon: Axis configuration coming in next update.
controls.noAxes: No axis bindings found for this device.
controls.ffbComingSoon: Force feedback editor coming in next update.
controls.ffbCurveHint: Double-click to add points. Right-click to remove. Drag to adjust.
controls.filtersComingSoon: Steering filter settings coming in next update.
controls.presetsComingSoon: Preset management coming in next update.

controls.bindActionWheel: Press a button or move an axis on your wheel...

controls.liveInputHint: Enable polling to see real-time input from your device.
controls.liveInputNoGamepad: No gamepad detected. Connect a controller and press a button.

adminTools.blurb: Community server admin and utility plugins for BeamMP. Browse and install with one click.

serverManager.noBackupsYet: No backups yet. Click "Backup Now" to create one.
serverManager.downloadDesc: This may take a moment. The download is running in the background.
serverManager.exeMissingDesc: A single server executable is shared by all instances. Download it once to run any server.
serverManager.directConnectTooltip: Connect to this server on your local machine
serverManager.directConnectSignIn: Sign in to BeamMP before connecting
serverManager.exeRequired: BeamMP-Server.exe is required to start the server
serverManager.portNotReachable: Port not reachable from the internet
serverManager.tailscaleTitle: Create a free VPN tunnel with Tailscale
serverManager.tailscaleStep1: Install Tailscale on your PC and your friends' PCs
serverManager.tailscaleStep2: Each person signs in — devices connect automatically
serverManager.tailscaleStep3: Share your Tailscale IP (shown in the Tailscale app) instead of your public IP
serverManager.tailscaleStep4: Friends use **Direct Connect** in BeamMP with your Tailscale IP
serverManager.tailscaleNote: No port forwarding needed — Tailscale punches through NAT for you.
serverManager.consoleEmpty: Console is empty — start the server to see output
serverManager.configDragOrClick: Drag & drop or click to select an image

careerMPConfig.title: CareerMP Configuration
careerMPConfig.blurb: Settings read from Resources/Server/CareerMP/config/config.json. Restart the server to apply.
careerMPConfig.missing: CareerMP is installed but config.json hasn't been generated yet. Start the server once so CareerMP creates the default config, then refresh this page.

dynamicTrafficConfig.title: BeamMP Dynamic Traffic Configuration
dynamicTrafficConfig.blurb: Settings read from Resources/Server/CareerMPTraffic/settings.txt. Use /traffic refresh in-game to apply without a restart.
dynamicTrafficConfig.missing: BeamMP Dynamic Traffic is installed but settings.txt hasn't been generated yet. Start the server once so the plugin creates the default config, then refresh this page.
dynamicTrafficConfig.aisPerPlayerHint: Each connected player contributes this many AI cars to the pool.
dynamicTrafficConfig.maxServerTrafficHint: Hard cap across all players combined.
dynamicTrafficConfig.adminsHint: Traffic admins are managed in-server via the console commands traffic.au / traffic.ru and are preserved on save.

serverManager.editorShortcuts: Ctrl+S save · Ctrl+F find · Ctrl+H replace
serverManager.deployTrackerTooltip: Deploy position tracker plugin to server
serverManager.undeployTrackerTooltip: Remove position tracker plugin from server
serverManager.deployVoicePluginTooltip: Deploy proximity voice chat plugin to server
serverManager.undeployVoicePluginTooltip: Remove voice chat plugin from server
serverManager.noDataForPeriod: No data for this period yet.
serverManager.undeployModMessage: Remove "{{fileName}}" from this server? This will delete the mod's client and server files from the server.
serverManager.deployModsDescription: Deploy installed mods to this server. Mods with server components will install both client and server files.
serverManager.noModsInstalled: No mods installed. Install mods from the Mods page first.
serverManager.tailscaleNotInstalledDesc: You'll need to install Tailscale first. It's free for personal use and takes about a minute.
serverManager.tailscaleNotConnectedDesc: Tailscale is installed but not running. Open the Tailscale app and sign in to connect.
serverManager.tailscaleGuideTitle: Host a server without port forwarding using Tailscale
serverManager.tailscaleGuideDesc: Tailscale creates a private mesh network between your devices. To let friends connect to your BeamMP server, you share your machine with them — they get access to just that machine, nothing else on your network. No port forwarding or router configuration needed.
serverManager.tailscaleHostStep1Suffix: and sign in with Google, Microsoft, or GitHub.
serverManager.tailscaleHostStep2Suffix: find your computer, click the ··· menu → Share, then send your friends an invite by email or copy a share link. Tip: use a reusable link if you have multiple friends.
serverManager.tailscaleHostStep3Desc: launch the server from this page like normal.
serverManager.tailscaleHostStep4Desc: give your friends your Tailscale IP and port
serverManager.tailscaleHostStep4Suffix: They'll use this to direct-connect in BeamMP.
serverManager.tailscaleFriendSteps: What your friends need to do
serverManager.tailscaleFriendStep2Desc: open the share link from your host (or check your email for the invite), sign in to Tailscale, and accept. You'll only get access to the host's server machine.
serverManager.tailscaleFriendStep3Desc: open BeamMP, click "Direct Connect", and enter your Tailscale IP and port
serverManager.tailscaleFreeNote: Tailscale is free for personal use (up to 100 devices, 3 users).
serverManager.tailscalePrivateNote: Shared machines are quarantined by default — friends can only reach the machine you shared, not anything else on your network.
serverManager.tailscaleEncryptedNote: Traffic is encrypted end-to-end and goes directly between devices (no relay servers).
serverManager.tailscaleLatencyNote: Connection quality is the same as a regular direct connection — no extra latency.
serverManager.tailscaleAutoNote: Tailscale runs in the background and auto-connects on boot.
serverManager.tailscaleShareLinkNote: You can create a reusable share link that up to 1,000 friends can use — great for gaming communities. Links expire after 30 days if unused.

settings.title: Settings
settings.languageDesc: Choose the language for the application interface
settings.defaultPortsDescription: Comma-separated ports or ranges. New server instances will use the next available port from this list.
settings.rendererDescription: Choose which graphics renderer to use when launching BeamNG.drive. Selecting DirectX 11 or Vulkan skips the launcher popup.
settings.supportToolsDescription: Troubleshooting tools for BeamNG.drive. These mirror the tools from the game's built-in launcher.
settings.openUserFolderDesc: Open the BeamNG.drive user data folder in Explorer.
settings.clearCacheDesc: Having problems with textures? Moves cache files to a backup folder.
settings.cacheClearedMB: Cache cleared ({{mb}} MB moved to backup)
settings.clearModCacheDesc: Delete cached BeamMP mod downloads. Mods will be re-downloaded from servers next join.
settings.modCacheClearedMB: Mod cache cleared ({{count}} files, {{mb}} MB freed)
settings.launchSafeModeDesc: Use a temporary blank user folder to isolate issues.
settings.launchSafeVulkanDesc: Temporary user folder with Vulkan renderer and debug logging.
settings.safeVulkanStarted: Game launched in safe + Vulkan mode
settings.verifyIntegrityDesc: Verify all game files are downloaded correctly via Steam.
settings.verifyTriggered: Steam verification started — check Steam for progress
settings.customExecutablesDesc: Point to a custom BeamMP server executable. Useful for modified builds. Clear to revert to the built-in default.
settings.uiScaleDesc: Scales the entire interface. 100% is the default (110% system zoom).
settings.blurEffectsDesc: Glassmorphism blur on panels and overlays
settings.serverListChunkSizeDesc: How many servers are rendered per chunk. Lower values keep scrolling smoother on huge lists; higher values mean fewer "load more" steps.
settings.resetDefaultsDesc: Restores all appearance settings to factory defaults.
settings.sidebarLayoutDesc: Drag to reorder sidebar items. Click the eye icon to show or hide items.
settings.customCSSWarningDesc: Custom CSS can break the UI or cause visual glitches. If the app becomes unusable, reset appearance from the settings or delete your config file.
settings.customCSSDesc: Write custom CSS to override any style in the application. Changes are applied live.
settings.customCSSHint: Tip: Use the browser DevTools (Ctrl+Shift+I) to inspect element classes and CSS variables.
settings.cssDisabledNotice: Custom CSS is saved but currently disabled. Toggle it on to apply.
settings.cssSnippetsHint: Toggle snippets on/off. Active snippets are highlighted.

setup.officialBackendDesc: Use the default BeamMP servers at backend.beammp.com
setup.selfHostedDesc: Connect to a custom BeamMP backend server

career.title: CareerMP
career.subtitle: Manage your CareerMP and RLS save files
career.noProfilesDescription: No CareerMP save data was found in your BeamNG.drive user directory. Start a career in-game to create save data.
career.deployDescription: Copy this save slot to another slot. This overwrites the target slot.
career.noData: No save data available for this slot
career.savePathDescription: The app auto-detects your BeamNG.drive save folder. If saves aren't found, browse to your saves directory manually.
career.noBackups: No backups found for this profile
career.deleteProfileConfirm: Permanently delete profile "{{name}}" and ALL its save slots? This cannot be undone.
career.deleteSlotConfirm: Permanently delete slot "{{slot}}" from profile "{{profile}}"? This cannot be undone.
career.deleteBackupFirstPrompt: Create a backup first so you can restore later if needed?

mod.careerMPBlurb: Enabling BeamNG.drive's Career Mode in BeamMP servers. Provides whatever handling is necessary on the BeamMP client and server to allow the base game career mode to function as multiplayer, so you can play career mode with friends in the same space. By StanleyDudek.
mod.careerMPNote: First-run setup: CareerMP ships as a server-side Lua plugin only. After installing, start the server once with no players connected — on first launch the server downloads the matching client mod zip into Resources/Client/. Stop the server, then start it again before letting players join. Skipping the restart will leave clients without the career UI/scripts.
mod.rlsBlurb: Making RLS Career Overhaul possible in multiplayer using CareerMP. This is a conversion of RLS that makes it possible to use RLS Career Overhaul with StanleyDudek's CareerMP. By PapiCheesecake.
mod.rlsNote: Picks the right CareerMP version for you, copies the RLS client zip into Resources/Client/ and writes the matching CareerMP server plugin. Like CareerMP, the server still needs the first-launch handshake to seed clients — start the server once after install, stop it, then start it again before players connect.

plugin.blurb: Curated CareerMP / RLS / BeamMP plugins. One-click install to the selected server.
plugin.beamngLoginRequired: Sign in to BeamNG.com to download this mod…

gps.title: Live GPS
gps.heading: Heading
gps.hint1: Deploy the tracker and launch BeamNG to see live position data.
gps.hint2: The tracker is a Lua extension placed in your user folder. Undeploy to remove it.

luaConsole.title: Dev Tools
luaConsole.deployed: Lua console extension deployed (port {{port}}). Launch or reload BeamNG to connect.
luaConsole.empty: No output yet — run something to see results here.

voiceChat.title: Voice Chat
voiceChat.infoBanner: Proximity voice chat uses WebRTC for peer-to-peer audio. The server must have the voice chat plugin installed. For CM-hosted servers, the plugin is auto-deployed.
voiceChat.gameNotRunningHint: Voice chat can only be enabled while BeamNG.drive is running. Launch the game first, then turn voice chat on — it will then auto-engage when you connect to a server.
voiceChat.testTransmitHint: Mic → Opus encode → jitter buffer → decode → speakers. Speak after pressing; you should hear yourself with ~180 ms delay. Click again to stop.
voiceChat.sensitivityHint: Lower = more sensitive (picks up quieter sounds). Higher = less sensitive.
voiceChat.proximityRangeHint: Maximum distance at which you can hear other players. Audio fades with distance.
voiceChat.doorMufflingDesc: Muffle voices when car doors are closed and camera is outside the vehicle
voiceChat.deployOverlayDesc: Auto-distribute the in-game voice overlay UI to every player joining your hosted servers (mic state, peer list, toggle button).
voiceChat.turnServerUrlHint: Optional. Required only for users behind symmetric NATs where STUN fails. Leave empty to use STUN only.
